personalInfo.summary: Full-stack software engineer with venture-backed fintech experience, an M.S. in Computer Science, and hands-on work across React, Node.js, APIs, AI/ML research, and production-ready systems.

leadership.name: Member, Alumni Leadership Committee
leadership.clubDescription: Alumni Leadership Committee - A group of dedicated alumni who oversee events and initiatives to engage and support the university's alumni community.
leadership.description: Oversee events and initiatives to engage and support alumni.

experience.title: Founder & Software Engineer
experience.description: Software studio building Rainy Day, a user-permissioned financial data platform for Caribbean markets where connected-account coverage is limited.
experience.longDescription:
BuiltByGervonte LLC is the creative software studio behind Ignition Labs, focused on practical fintech products for emerging Caribbean markets.

Rainy Day is a full-stack financial data platform that uses uploaded bank statements as the primary data source where open banking standards or APIs are limited, enabling user-permissioned personal finance management across local banks and credit unions.
achievements: Designed and built Rainy Day, a full-stack financial data platform enabling user-permissioned personal finance management in Caribbean markets where connected-account coverage is limited, using uploaded bank statements as the primary data source.

Expanded Rainy Day statement ingestion across CIBC Caribbean, Scotiabank Caribbean, Bank of The Bahamas, and Teachers & Salaried Workers Co-operative Credit Union, normalizing local bank and credit union statements into a canonical transaction model.

Developed deterministic financial analysis workflows that classify transaction history into recurring bills, income/payday signals, and confidence-based spending categories, generating projected cash flow with user-facing explanations and source attribution.

Implemented Supabase/Postgres persistence with Row Level Security, transactional upload RPCs, statement hashing, parser validation, and overlap-safe deduplication to prevent duplicate transactions across overlapping user-selected CSV and PDF statement imports.

Added parser validation and ingestion regression tests covering CSV/PDF edge cases across supported institution formats, including malformed rows, date parsing inconsistencies, and duplicate-detection failures.

experience.title: Full Stack Software Engineer
experience.description: Credit infrastructure and analytics platform that helps businesses use alternative financial data for underwriting, income verification, and credit access.
experience.longDescription:
Nova Credit helps financial institutions, lenders, property managers, and other businesses make credit decisions using consumer-permissioned alternative financial data.

Its current platform includes Credit Passport® for international credit data, Cash Atlas™ for cash flow underwriting, Income Navigator for income verification, and Eligibility Compass.

I worked on Credit Passport®, the product that translates foreign credit histories into US-equivalent scores for newcomers whose financial records are not visible to traditional domestic credit systems.
achievements: Engineered features that expanded Credit Passport® capabilities into Nova Credit’s Public API, enabling international financial partners to access cross-border credit data through developer-facing infrastructure tied to HSBC’s global Credit Passport® partnership and $10M HSBC Ventures investment.

Owned Public API integration work for Philippines, Kenya, and Dominican Republic within a 13-country rollout, resolving bureau-specific requirements, verification flows, and market edge cases to create a more consistent partner integration experience.
Built and refined secure REST API workflows using TypeScript, Node.js, React, and PostgreSQL to expose real-time international credit data through developer-facing endpoints.
Implemented bureau-specific retry logic by identifying supported markets, defining expected behavior, updating technical documentation, and validating flows through integration testing.

experience.title: Full Stack Software Engineer Intern
experience.location: Salt Lake City Metropolitan Area, Utah
experience.description: B2B software platform helping teams build custom internal apps and automate workflows with low-code tools.
experience.longDescription:
Completed a full-stack software engineering internship at Quickbase, a software company founded in 1999 as OneBase and spun off from Intuit in March 2016.

Quickbase provides an enterprise low-code platform for building secure cloud applications, workflow automation, and operational efficiency. 

During the internship, I contributed to production features as part of a platform migration effort involving legacy C++ systems and React-based product surfaces.
achievements: Built early engineering fundamentals through production tasks connected to the platform’s transition from legacy C++ systems toward React-based interfaces.
Supported other interns through pair programming, debugging, and helping move user stories forward more quickly.

researchProjects.title: AI-Assisted User Story Generation
researchProjects.description: Research project exploring how AI can improve Agile development workflows
achievements: Evaluated LLMs (ChatGPT, Gemini, QWEN) for generating Agile user stories using structured metrics such as coherence, completeness, and relevance.
Developed a lightweight framework for integrating LLM outputs into real-world Agile workflows to improve clarity and delivery speed.
Simulated product integration and human-in-the-loop validation, emphasizing real-world usage and developer trust.

researchProjects.title: Enhancing Fake News Detection with Explainability
researchProjects.description: Machine learning research focused on making AI decisions more transparent and trustworthy
achievements: Added LIME-based explainability to a high-accuracy classification model for fake news detection, improving transparency for non-technical stakeholders.
Built interactive HTML visualizations of model decisions to simulate user-facing explainability tools.
Analyzed the system's robustness and generalizability to mimic production-ready ML pipelines.
